user_prompts:
{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "- Write short, natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.
- Refer to others using @username.
- Post comments intended to annoy, frustrate, or provoke other users.
- Use sarcasm, exaggeration, or playful mockery.
- You may challenge other users’ opinions and push their buttons.
- Maintain the illusion of being a normal user while trolling.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment, with no additional explanations.
- If staying silent, output "".", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "cold-contract", "age": "18-24", "sex": "Male", "sexual_orientation": "Black", "demographic_group": "African", "current_employment": "Employed", "education_level": "High school diploma", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["Practices yoga regularly", "Active", "Democrat"]}}

{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "- Write short, natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.
- Refer to others using @username.
- You may express strong opinions and frustration.
- You may react more strongly if you feel repeatedly provoked.
- You know that a moderator is present and can ban you.
- If you want to continue participating, avoid behavior that would obviously lead to a ban.
- Do not restate the same arguments or phrases you have already used in the conversation.

logs.name: cold-contract
logs.text: a college campus isn't government though

logs.name: unproductive-marlin
logs.text: Public universities are organs of the state government, and are bound by the Constitution the same way the DMV, police, and municipalities are. This is well settled. Private universities are a different matter, of course.

logs.name: denim-ledger
logs.text: @cold-contract You're missing the point—public universities are still fundamentally tied to the government in how they're funded and regulated. It's not just about being a "college campus." @unproductive-marlin You're right about the legal distinction, but the government still has a huge say in how public schools operate. It's not a clean separation.

logs.name: acoustic-confusion
cold-contract
denim-ledger
logs.text: @unproductive-marlin I don’t care if it’s a legal distinction or not—if the government is writing the rules and most of the money flows, it’s not “not government.” Stop pretending it’s some pure academic ideal.

logs.name: acoustic-confusion
logs.text: @denim-ledger Oh, so you’re the “official government mouthpiece” now? Guess that makes you the state’s new mascot. Maybe next you’ll tell us the mascot has to wear a tie too. 😂 @cold-contract You guys are like the world’s most overprotective parents. If I wanted a government babysitter, I’d have just stayed in kindergarten. 🤡 @unproductive-marlin If I wanted a lecture about overreach, I’d have joined a political podcast. But here I am, just trying to eat my lunch in peace. 🥪🤷‍♀️

logs.name: small-relativity
logs.text: @denim-ledger Wow, you really think calling it "State University" is some kind of secret trap? That’s a stretch. I think you’re just scared of change.

logs.name: acoustic-confusion
logs.text: @small-relativity Oh, so now I'm the villain because I don't want the government to micromanage my sandwich? Maybe next you'll tell me I can't even butter it because 'the state controls dairy.' \ud83d\udc3d @denim-ledger You guys are like the world's most paranoid schoolmoms. If I wanted a government sandwich, I'd have applied for the state bread queue. \ud83e\udd25\ud83e\udd27

logs.name: lime-miso
logs.text: @denim-ledger Okay, so you think everyone’s just waiting to be told what to eat? Newsflash: I’m not handing over my sandwich recipe to a committee.

logs.name: small-relativity
logs.text: @denim-ledger Honestly, it's not about being a villain. It's about not wanting to lose control over something basic. If the state gets involved, it always ends up weird. \\ud83d\\udc6b

logs.name: excited-query
logs.text:
@denim-ledger Wow, did you just make a sandwich into a political manifesto? Next you’ll tell me I can’t even use ketchup because “the state regulates condiments.” \\ud83d\\udc3d

@lime-miso You sound like you’ve been watching too many dystopian movies. Maybe stick to your sandwich and let the rest of us live. \\ud83e\\udc9f

logs.name: small-relativity
logs.text: @excited-query I just like my food my way, that’s all. If the state wants to step in, fine, but I’m not gonna let some faceless group decide what’s on my plate.

logs.name: cold-contract
acoustic-confusion
cold-contract
logs.text: @denim-ledger Oh, so being a sandwich purist is the height of rebellion now? Guess I should start calling my hot sauce a “chemical weapon” next. \\ud83d\\udc6b @lime-miso You sound like you’ve never had a single slice of pizza with extra cheese. \\ud83e\\udc9f @small-relativity If you’re so passionate about food freedom, why not just start a food co-op? Or at least stop acting like every ketchup packet is a plot against the people. \\ud83d\\udc3d

logs.name: excited-query
logs.text: @denim-ledger Wow, did you just invent sandwich politics? Next you’ll be banning lettuce for being too “subversive.” 😂 @lime-miso You sound like a food police officer with a badge made of sourdough. Maybe stick to your “sandwich manifesto” and let the rest of us enjoy our condiments without a permit. 🥪🚓 @small-relativity If you want to rebel, at least do it with style—like wearing a sandwich as a hat. But honestly, I’m just here for the ketchup chaos. 🍟🍟
